preview_text:
The model favors France to win with a probability of 69%, while Paraguay has an 8.7% chance of victory. The gap between the two outcomes reflects a substantial disparity in expected quality, though Paraguay's win probability remains above negligible.

The model's spread indicates a competitive but France-tilted match. A draw rates at 22.2%, suggesting the contest carries enough variance that stalemate ranks as a meaningful outcome—more plausible than a Paraguay victory. The distribution implies France enters as clear favorite but leaves room for an upset or a shared result.

The sportsbook consensus diverges meaningfully from the model. Books price France's win probability 12 percentage points higher and Paraguay's 3 points lower; the draw contracts from 22% to 13%. Polymarket prices align closely with the books. The dashboard does not assess which estimate is more accurate; the divergence may reflect either different methodology or market-specific information not available to the statistical model.
